plainEnglish: At Level AAA, use headings to organize content into labeled sections. A clear heading structure (properly nested <h1>–<h6>) lets people scan and jump to the part they need, especially in long documents.
whyItMatters: Screen reader users (and anyone skimming) navigate long content by jumping between headings; without section headings they must read everything linearly.
quickCheck: Confirm long content is broken into sections with descriptive headings and that heading levels nest logically with no skipped levels.
commonMistakes: Long pages with no headings to break up the content.
Using headings for visual styling rather than to mark real sections.

codeExamples.label: A wall of text with no section headings
codeExamples.code:
{/* One long block with nothing to navigate by */}
